title: Mise à jour du suivi du risque de récession mondiale — dim. 12 avr. 2026
summary: Les marchés ont clôturé pour le week-end de Pâques avec le S&P 500 en hausse d'environ +3 % sur la semaine du 7 au 11 avril — une surface trompeusement haussière masquant de graves tensions sous-jacentes.

title: Global Recession Risk Tracker Update — Sat Apr 11, 2026
summary: IMF Spring Meetings Open April 13: Georgieva 'Cushioning the Middle East War Shock' — WEO April 2026 to Show 'Notable Markdowns'; IMF Prepares $50B Emergency Assistance.

title: Global Recession Risk Tracker Update — Fri Apr 10, 2026
summary: The Bureau of Labor Statistics released the March 2026 Consumer Price Index on April 10, revealing the first full month of price data since the US-Israel-Iran conflict began on February 28. Michigan Consumer Sentiment Plunges to All-Time Record Low of 47.6; Year-Ahead Inflation Expectations Spike to 4.8% — Largest One-Month Jump in Survey History.

title: Breaking: IMF Warns of Looming Global Inflation Crisis Driven by US-Israel War on Iran
summary: IMF warns of looming global inflation crisis driven by the US-Israel war on Iran, adding a major multilateral institution's assessment to existing stagflation and recession risk signals.

title: Global Recession Risk Update — Apr 9, 2026
summary: A historic dual tariff shock hit global markets April 9 as Trump imposed an additional 50% tariff on all Chinese goods (bringing the effective rate to ~104%) and China raised its retaliation to 84% on US imports — the deepest bilateral trade barrier since the 1930s Smoot-Hawley era. JPMorgan raised its US recession probability to 65% (highest since pandemic) and Moody's Analytics crossed the 50% threshold. This follows the April 7 US-Iran two-week ceasefire that briefly sent the Dow surging 1,325 points (+2.85%) and WTI crude crashing 16.4% to $94.41 — the biggest single-day oil decline since April 2020. The ceasefire remains fragile (Lebanon strikes continue; Iran and US proposals conflict on key terms). The Cleveland Fed nowcast projects March CPI at 3.25%, deepening the stagflation bind ahead of the critical FOMC April 29–30 meeting. Goldman Sachs lowered its 2026 US GDP forecast to 1.4%.

title: Global Recession Risk Tracker Update — Wed Apr 8, 2026
summary: The day after the two-week US-Iran ceasefire was announced, multiple signs of fragility emerged rapidly. Trump Issues Ultimatum to China: Withdraw 34% Retaliation or Face Additional 50% Tariff Bringing Total to 104%; Beijing Says 'Fight to the End'.

title: Global Recession Risk Tracker Update — Tue Apr 7, 2026
summary: A two-week US-Iran ceasefire announced barely an hour before President Trump's April 8 'obliterate' deadline triggered the most dramatic single-day market rally since April 2025. Despite the Iran ceasefire market relief, analysts on April 7 flagged unresolved stagflation signals embedded in the real-time economic data.

title: Global Recession Risk Update — Apr 6, 2026
summary: The largest two-day stock market loss in history ($6.6 trillion) unfolded April 3–4 after Trump expanded tariffs on steel, aluminum, and copper (50%) and announced 100% pharmaceutical tariffs on Liberation Day's one-year anniversary (April 2). China immediately responded with a 34% retaliatory tariff on all US goods effective April 10, triggering a further crash (S&P -10% from peak, Nasdaq in bear market). The March jobs report (BLS, Apr 3) showed 178K payrolls added but February was revised to a net loss of 92,000 — the first monthly payroll decline since 2020. Oil peaked at $113/barrel before the Muscat Protocol (Iran-Oman, Apr 5) opened a 'Green Channel' for Strait of Hormuz commercial shipping, providing partial relief. Markets partially stabilized April 6 with VIX retreating to 24.54. JPMorgan maintains 60% recession probability; Goldman Sachs raised its estimate to 35%; the Fed faces a stagflationary bind with core PCE at 3.06% ahead of the April 29–30 FOMC.

title: Global Recession Risk Tracker Update — Sun Apr 5, 2026
summary: Muscat Protocol: Iran-Oman Sign Strait of Hormuz Shipping Agreement; 'Green Channel' Opens for Commercial Traffic.

title: Global Recession Risk Tracker Update — Sat Apr 4, 2026
summary: China Imposes 34% Retaliatory Tariff on All US Goods; $6.6 Trillion Two-Day Market Loss — Largest in History. As the tariff crash unfolded on April 4, oil markets reached their peak stress level from the overlapping Iran conflict.

title: Global Recession Risk Tracker Update — Fri Apr 3, 2026
summary: Thursday April 3 saw the first full session of the tariff-triggered market crash, with US equities posting their worst single-day performance since the COVID-19 pandemic crash of 2020. The Bureau of Labor Statistics released the March 2026 Employment Situation Summary on April 3.

title: Global Recession Risk Tracker Update — Thu Apr 2, 2026
summary: April 2, 2026 marked the one-year anniversary of 'Liberation Day' (April 2, 2025), when the Trump administration announced sweeping reciprocal tariffs. Multiple economic analyses released on the one-year anniversary of Liberation Day (April 2, 2025) assessed the tariff regime's record.

title: Global Recession Risk Update — Apr 1, 2026
summary: JPMorgan raised its US recession probability to 60% — the highest of any major investment bank — as Liberation Day approached its one-year anniversary with $166 billion in IEEPA tariff revenue under legal threat and the tariff regime widely assessed as having failed its own metrics. China's NBS Manufacturing PMI surged back to expansion at 50.4 in March (first expansion since March 2025), though input prices hit a 4-year high from Iran-conflict commodity costs. President Trump opened his first China visit since 2017 on April 1 in Beijing, with the Trump-Xi summit focusing on agriculture purchases, rare earth concessions, and fentanyl enforcement amid China's record $213.6 billion trade surplus. Markets rallied on Iran ceasefire signals (S&P +0.7%, Brent crude falling to $98.79/barrel, 10Y yield retreating to ~4.30%), while Conference Board Consumer Confidence Expectations remained at 70.9 — the 14th consecutive month below the 80-point recession-warning threshold. Core PCE re-accelerated to 3.06% year-over-year in January 2026 BEA data (released March 13).

title: Global Recession Risk Tracker Update — Tue Mar 31, 2026
summary: China NBS Manufacturing PMI Jumps to 50.4 — First Expansion Since March 2025; Input Prices Surge to 4-Year High. The Conference Board Consumer Confidence Index for March 2026 came in at 91.8 (1985=100), marginally above February's 91.0 and slightly beating the Reuters consensus of 88.0. The final session of March 2026 crystallized a difficult month for risk assets.

title: Global Recession Risk Tracker Update — Mon Mar 30, 2026
summary: Bloomberg published a newsletter retrospective on March 30, one week before the first anniversary of Liberation Day (April 2, 2025). CNN Business published an analysis titled 'A recession is guaranteed.

title: Global Recession Risk Update — Mar 29, 2026
summary: Stagflation risk surged in the final week of March as recession probability estimates approached multi-year highs: Moody's Analytics at 48.6%, Goldman Sachs at 30%, J.P. Morgan at 35%, and EY-Parthenon at 40%. The US 10-year Treasury yield reached 4.46% on March 28 — its highest since July 2025 — as bond markets fully priced out 2026 Fed rate cuts and began pricing a 50% probability of a hike by October. University of Michigan consumer sentiment deteriorated to a final March reading of 53.3, with year-ahead inflation expectations surging to 3.8% (the largest monthly jump since April 2025). On March 26, the EU Parliament advanced the 'Turnberry' EU-US trade framework 417-154, while China opened two retaliatory trade investigations into US practices (March 27) in response to the USTR Section 301 probes. The S&P 500 posted its fifth consecutive weekly loss — the longest streak since 2022 — and the US dollar recorded its best month since July 2025 as safe-haven demand surged.

title: Global Recession Risk Tracker Update — Sat Mar 28, 2026
summary: US 10-Year Treasury Yield Hits 4.46% — Highest Since July 2025. US Dollar Posts Best Month Since July 2025; DXY Above 100 as Safe-Haven Demand Surges.

title: Global Recession Risk Tracker Update — Fri Mar 27, 2026
summary: Michigan Consumer Sentiment Final: 53.3, Year-Ahead Inflation Expectations Jump to 3.8%. China Opens Two Trade Investigations Into US Practices in Retaliatory Signal.

title: Global Recession Risk Update — Mar 26, 2026
summary: Recession risk escalated sharply in the week of March 19–26, with Wall Street probability estimates reaching 30–49%: Moody's Analytics at 48.6%, EY Parthenon at 40%, J.P. Morgan at 35%, and Goldman Sachs at 30%. The Federal Reserve held rates steady at 3.50–3.75% on March 18, with the dot plot projecting only one cut in 2026 and core PCE revised up to 2.7% year-end, complicating any near-term easing. Consumer sentiment hit its lowest reading of 2026 (University of Michigan 55.5, 2nd percentile historically) as gasoline price shock from the Iran conflict erased earlier gains. On March 11, USTR launched sweeping Section 301 investigations into excess manufacturing capacity across 16 economies — including China, EU, Mexico, and India — targeting tariff imposition by July 2026, while Penn Wharton confirmed the current effective US tariff rate at 10.3%, the highest since 1943. Analysis published March 19 concluded that by the administration's own metrics, the tariff regime has failed to narrow the trade deficit or make foreign producers pay, with JPMorgan estimating pass-through to US consumers accelerating in 2026.

title: Global Recession Risk Tracker Update — Wed Mar 25, 2026
summary: CNBC reported that recession probability estimates from major Wall Street institutions have reached elevated levels: Moody's Analytics 48.6%, Wilmington Trust 45%, EY Parthenon 40%, J.P. Analysis: Trump Tariffs Have Failed by Their Own Metrics — Trade Deficit Widened, Not Narrowed.

title: Global Recession Risk Tracker Update — Tue Mar 24, 2026
summary: Bloomberg reported on March 24 that the US Treasury bond market was approaching an 'inflection point' after a sustained selloff driven by oil-price inflation fears from the Iran conflict. S&P Global's preliminary US Manufacturing PMI for March 2026 rose to 52.4, up from 51.6 in February, beating the 51.3 consensus estimate.

title: Global Recession Risk Tracker Update — Mon Mar 23, 2026
summary: US trade envoy warned the European Union on March 23 that Brussels should expect elevated tariffs if negotiations over the 'Turnberry' EU-US trade framework fail to produce a deal.

title: Global Recession Risk Tracker Update — Thu Mar 19, 2026
summary: IMF: 'Coping and Thriving in a Fluid World' — Downside Risks Rising, Resilience Tested.

title: Global Recession Risk Tracker Update — Wed Mar 18, 2026
summary: The FOMC voted to hold the federal funds rate at 3.50–3.75% at its March 17–18 meeting, the second consecutive pause.

title: Global Recession Risk Tracker Update — Mon Mar 16, 2026
summary: Penn Wharton Budget Model updated its effective tariff rate (ETR) estimate to 10.3% through January 2026. Formal USMCA review talks commenced on March 16, 2026, coinciding with the USTR's Section 301 investigations into Canada and Mexico.

title: Global Recession Risk Tracker Update — Fri Mar 13, 2026
summary: The University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment Index fell to 55.5 in March 2026 (preliminary), down 1.9% from 56.6 in February, hitting its lowest level of the year. BEA data confirmed US real GDP grew just 0.7% in Q4 2025, down sharply from 4.4% in Q3 2025, a 3.7 percentage point deceleration.

title: Global Recession Risk Tracker Update — Wed Mar 11, 2026
summary: USTR Launches Section 301 Investigations into Excess Manufacturing Capacity Across 16 Economies.
